word_list.word: a cracked bell can never sound well

senses.definition: used to imply that something that is damaged or flawed will never work properly, regardless of its other qualities or potential, as the flaw can significantly impact its effectiveness

examples.x: The writer's grammatical errors and poor sentence structure made it challenging for readers to understand their ideas, proving that a cracked bell can never sound well.

senses.definition: used to suggest that something of high value, even with a minor imperfection, is more desirable than something ordinary or mundane that is perfect

word_list.word: a good tale is none the worse for being told twice
word_list.pronunciation: …ź …°ňą äd tňąe…™l …™z nňą Ćn √į…ô wňą…úňźs f…Ēňź…Ļ bňĆiňź…™Ňč tňąo äld twňąa…™s

senses.definition: used to suggest that a good story is timeless and can be enjoyed and appreciated regardless of how many times it is repeated
used to imply that opting for the least expensive option can end up being the most expensive in the long run, as it may require further expenses to fix problems or replace low-quality goods

examples.x: I thought I was getting a good deal on this used car, but the constant repairs and maintenance costs have made it clear that cheapest is dearest.

senses.definition: used to suggest that starting with high-quality materials or resources leads to better outcomes and long-term success

examples.x: The business owner understood that hiring talented employees was essential for a successful company, as good seed makes a good crop.

word_list.pronunciation: …ź …Ēňź…Ļ …°ňą äd wňąa…™n nňąiňźdz nňąo ä bňą ä É

senses.definition: used to suggest that something of high quality or value does not require advertising or promotion, as its excellence is apparent

examples.x: The salesman confidently presented his product, knowing that good wine needs no bush.

word_list.word: if you want (something|a thing) done (right|well), (you might as well|) do it yourself

senses.definition: used to suggest that to ensure a task is completed to a high standard, it is best to undertake it oneself rather than relying on others

word_list.word: if (a thing|it) is worth doing, it is worth doing well

senses.definition: used to imply that important tasks or activities deserve one's full effort and attention to ensure a successful outcome
used to imply that small or simple things can be just as significant and admirable as large or complex things, emphasizing that worth or quality is not necessarily linked to size or complexity

examples.x: The small, family-owned restaurant may not have an elaborate menu, but the food is delicious and made with care, proving that small is beautiful and emphasizing that something small can be of high quality and value.

senses.definition: used to war that it is difficult to achieve both speed and high quality simultaneously, suggesting that a focus on one may come at the expense of the other

examples.x: The software developer focused on creating high-quality code, rather than rushing to meet a deadline, acknowledging that good and quickly seldom meet and recognizing that poor-quality software could lead to additional expenses and problems.

senses.definition: used to suggest that something small can have great value or significance, and that one should not judge the worth of something based solely on its size or outward appearance

word_list.word: jack of all trades is a master of none
word_list.pronunciation: d íňą√¶k  Ćv ňą…Ēňźl t…Ļňąe…™dz …™z …ź mňą√¶st…ö…Ļ  Ćv nňą Ćn

senses.definition: used to imply that a person who tries to do many different things may not become excellent or an expert in any one thing

examples.x: He had dabbled in many different fields but recognized that a jack of all trades is a master of none, so he decided to focus on one area and become a true expert.

senses.definition: used to imply that something that is old or of poor quality is often more durable or long-lasting than something new or of better quality

examples.x: The old radio had been in the family for generations and was still working, providing clear sound and reminding the family that a creaking door hangs longest.
